summary: Good‑sounding soundboard capture of the 1987 Dylan‑with‑the‑Dead show, highlighted by standout performances of Slow Train Coming, Joey and a rousing Watchtower.
review: The set showcases a hot‑day concert where the Dead and Bob Dylan click on several numbers – especially “Slow Train Coming,” “Joey” and an exuberant take on “All Along The Watchtower.”  Reviewers note the soundboard source is generally clear, though mid‑80s Dolby C noise reduction adds a slight processed sheen.  Despite a grumpy crowd early on, the band’s energy and tight playing make it a memorable performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (average ~4.2) and enthusiastic comments about the music outweigh minor recording coloration concerns.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced, but reviewers note mid‑80s Dolby C noise reduction giving a slightly processed/colored tone.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A single long “megaset” followed by a dedicated Dylan segment; the heat and early crowd restlessness gave way to strong grooves, especially on the Dylan numbers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is praised (“ripped it up”) and his vocal harmonies behind Dylan are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal performance on “All Along The Watchtower” is singled out as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (Barlow) provides notable backing vocals on the opening of the Dylan set.
